channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-981] lustre client crash when accessing a lustre file mounted as loopback
item.description:
To increase performance using small files, as several lustre guides recommend, we created a "big" file to use it mounted as loopback. When you write some data the client stops and the whole computer hangs.

    • It works using version 1.8.5!!!

Example:

dd if=/dev/zero of=/lustre21/my_fs_personal.iso bs=8192 count=9999
mkfs.ext4 /lustre21/my_fs_personal.iso

mount -t ext4 -o loop /lustre21/mi_fs_personal.iso /mnt

dd if=/dev/zero of=/mnt/example_file.dat bs=8192 count=8999
item.summary: lustre client crash when accessing a lustre file mounted as loopback

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment:
Integrated in lustre-dev » x86_64,client,el6,inkernel #340
LU-981 llite: fix loop back device writeback (Revision 56e56ed49fc5392048872e4b9502a4ea66be2d90)

Result = SUCCESS
Oleg Drokin : 56e56ed49fc5392048872e4b9502a4ea66be2d90
Files :

  • lustre/llite/lloop.c
  • lustre/llite/vvp_io.c

Files :

  • lustre/llite/lloop.c
  • lustre/llite/vvp_io.c

Hello,

First of all, than you for your support

a) Using lctl blockdev_attach <lustre-file> <loop-device-node> instead
of mount works perfect under lustre 2.1.0

b) About your patch:

  • We tested against the source from

http://downloads.whamcloud.com/public/lustre/lustre-2.1.0/el6/client/x86_64/

But we cannot compile it because it fails:

/home/XXXXXX/lustre_client/usr/src/lustre-2.1.0/lustre/llite/lloop.c:754:
error: implicit declaration of function ‘blkdev_get_by_dev’

If we apply ourselves directly the changes into the source code (without
using patch) compiles and it works.

Works perfect!

After using this patch into the source code, works using ctl
blockdev_attach and using mount -o loop!!!!

Thank you.

Best Regards,

Gabriel

–
RDlab (Campus Nord - UPC) – http://rdlab.lsi.upc.edu
C/ Jordi Girona 1-3. Edifici Omega, Despatx 005
08034 Barcelona

Telèfon: +34 93 413 78 20
yes, I think so.

Hello,

Thank you, we will check this patch and tell you what happended.

If it works, will be included in 2.1.1?

Best regards,

Gabriel

–
RDlab (Campus Nord - UPC) – http://rdlab.lsi.upc.edu
C/ Jordi Girona 1-3. Edifici Omega, Despatx 005
08034 Barcelona

Telèfon: +34 93 413 78 20

Please try patch: http://review.whamcloud.com/#change,2010

I'd like to suggest a new way for you guys to use loop device:
1. insmod llite_lloop.ko
2. lctl blockdev_attach <lustre-file> <loop-device-node>
3. mkfs.ext4 <loop-device-node>

This should have better performance and use less memory on the client side.
Jinshan, please take a look at this, seems to be clio related.

Hello,

After writing on the mounting file, system crash. This is the coredump
left on the console/log files of the client.

best regards,

Gabriel

Hello,

After writing on the file, the client "hangs". This is the onycoredump
left in the log/console screen:

Best regards,

Gabriel

Hello,

After writing on the file, the client "hangs". This is the onycoredump left in the log/console screen:

Best regards,

Gabriel
